title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · Recovery Case: Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref
summary: A release repository uses symbolic refs, detached work, replace refs, ambiguous names, and complex revision sets; a maintainer must answer an audit question without moving any ref. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita ref symbolic pseudoref increment in repository-state and revision-query proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G31 is a theory in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref while producing repository-state and revision-query proof.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G31 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip. Competency probe: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence. A defensible solution applies Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to repository-state and revision-query proof.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-ref-symbolic-pseudoref — Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip
git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts
# expected: left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference
# observation: two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set
# changed-case: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip
# transfer-limit: verify revision parsing against installed Git help before applying the query to a real repository
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-ref-symbolic-pseudoref-148ulz" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-ref-symbolic-pseudoref-148ulz
# scenario-key: trace-148ulz probe-zlu841 boundary-8ulz14 owner-148ulzx fault-148ulzz
# before-ref: main=a11ce14 candidate=b00b1lz
# hypothesis: the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip
git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts
# expected: left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference
# observation: two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set
# changed-case: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry
# after-ref: main=a11ce14 candidate=c0ffeez
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip
# transfer-limit: verify revision parsing against installed Git help before applying the query to a real repository
